task_desc: Fix remaining GUS_GRN_* gustatory neuron classification mismatch missed by D18.2, and fix highlight region abrupt snap-back on timer expiry -- Two residual issues from the D18.x fixes: (1) D18.2 fixed classification mismatches for ANTENNAL_MECH and DN_*/VNC_CPG but missed three gustatory neurons: GUS_GRN_SWEET, GUS_GRN_BITTER, and GUS_GRN_WATER are classified as sensory in BRAIN.neuronRegions (connectome.js:104) but are placed in the Subesophageal Zone region which has type central in both brain3d.js (line 75, REGION_DEFS) and education.js (line 57, EDUCATION_REGIONS); users viewing both panels simultaneously see these neurons as blue (sensory) dots in the connectome panel but inside a purple (central) region in the 3D brain; fix by moving GUS_GRN_SWEET, GUS_GRN_BITTER, GUS_GRN_WATER from the sensory array to the central array in connectome.js BRAIN.neuronRegions, matching the brain3d/education classification and the same approach used in D18.2 (the SEZ functionally integrates gustatory input so central classification is defensible). (2) D18.1 fixed the highlight feature so update() skips highlighted regions, but when the 1200ms _highlightUntil timer expires (brain3d.js:322-327), the region snaps abruptly from highlight values (opacity 0.9, emissiveIntensity 1.5) to calculated activation values in a single frame with no interpolation; when activation is low this produces a jarring flash-to-dark transition; fix by adding a brief fade-out period (e.g. 300ms) after _highlightUntil expires where opacity and emissiveIntensity linearly interpolate from highlight values toward the calculated target values. Files: js/connectome.js, js/brain3d.js.
